request.question: In between The Postal Service and Chagall Guevara which one was formed in 1989?

title: Afghan Post
Postage stamps and postal history of Poland
Chagall Guevara (album)
United States Postal Inspection Service
Army Postal Service (India)
Chagall Guevara
SpeedPost
Canada Post
Steve Taylor & The Perfect Foil
The Postal Service
sentences: Afghan Post is the national post office of Afghanistan.
It is responsible for providing postal service in Afghanistan.
Afghanistan first established a postal service in 1870, where it received international recognition.
On the eve of the Soviet invasion it had grown into one of the stronger regional postal services, able to send and receive letters from anywhere in the world in a timely manner.
During the 1990s the Afghan postal service lay in near total ruin, undone by the nation's civil war.
Sending a letter usually meant having to find someone traveling in the direction of the recipient willing to carry a note and hoping for the best.
Since the civil war, the postal service has reinvented itself with offices in all 34 provinces, and, it is close to having offices in all 364 districts.

As the government struggles to develop despite an excess of foreign aid, the post office has quietly managed to become one of the most efficient national institutions – and with extremely limited international assistance.
Though street addresses are a foreign concept in Afghanistan, the postal service manages to do house deliveries.
The Afghan postal service has turned into a promising administration.
Poczta Polska, the Polish postal service, was founded in 1558 and postal markings were first introduced in 1764.
The three partitions of Poland in 1772, 1793 and 1795 saw the independent nation of Poland disappear.
The postal services in the areas occupied by Germany and Austria were absorbed into those countries' postal services.
In 1772 the area occupied by Austria was created into the Kingdom of Galicia, a part of the Austrian Empire.
This lasted till 1918.
The Duchy of Warsaw was created briefly, between 1807 and 1813, by Napoleon I of France, from Polish lands ceded by the Kingdom of Prussia under the terms of the Treaties of Tilsit.
In 1815, following Napoleons’ defeat in 1813, the Congress of Vienna, created Congress Poland out of the Duchy of Warsaw and also established the Free City of Kraków.
Congress Poland was placed under the control of Russia and the postal service was given autonomy in 1815.
In 1851 the postal service was put under the control of the Russian post office department regional office in St Petersburg.
In 1855 control was restored for a while to the Congress Kingdom but following the uprising in 1863 again came under Russian control from 1866 and continued until World War I.
In November 1918 the Second Polish Republic was created.
Chagall Guevara is the title of the only full-length album by the band Chagall Guevara, released in 1991, on MCA Records.
The United States Postal Inspection Service (USPIS) is the law enforcement arm of the United States Postal Service.
Its jurisdiction is defined as "crimes that may adversely affect or fraudulently use the U.S. Mail, the postal system or postal employees."

The mission of the U.S. Postal Inspection Service is to support and protect the U.S. Postal Service, its employees, infrastructure, and customers by enforcing the laws that defend the nation’s mail system from illegal or dangerous use.
The Army Postal Service (APS), functions as a government-operated military mail system in India.

A primary feature of Army Postal Service systems is that normally they are subsidized to ensure that military mail posted between duty stations abroad and the home country (or vice versa) does not cost the sender any more than normal domestic mail traffic.

In some cases, Indian military personnel in a combat zone may post letters and/or packages to the home country for free, while in others, senders located in a specific overseas area may send military mail to another military recipient, also located in the same overseas area, without charge.
Officers are deputed primarily from the Indian Postal Service and from the Army Corps.
Army Postal Service is the only service that enables civil service officers to work in the armed forces.

Chagall Guevara was an American rock band formed in 1989 by solo artist Steve Taylor, guitarists Dave Perkins and Lynn Nichols (from the 1970s Phil Keaggy band), bassist Wade Jaynes, and drummer Mike Mead.
SpeedPost or Speed Post is the trade name or brand name for a high-speed postal service provided by several postal companies around the world.

It is Taiwan Post's high-speed domestic postal service in Taiwan, Hongkong Post's international courier post service (with the generic name "EMS"), India Post's local and international postal as well as money transfer service, as well as SingPost's local and international express and trackable postal and courier service

Canada Post Corporation, known more simply as Canada Post (French: "Société Canadienne des Postes" , or simply "Postes Canada"), is a Crown corporation which functions as the country's primary postal operator.

Originally known as Royal Mail Canada (the operating name of the Post Office Department of the Canadian government founded in 1867), rebranding was done to the “Canada Post” name in the late 1960s, even though it had not yet been separated from the government.
On October 16, 1981, the Canada Post Corporation Act came into effect.
This abolished the Post Office Department and created the present day Crown corporation which provides postal service.
The act aimed to set a new direction for the postal service by ensuring the postal service's financial security and independence.

Steve Taylor & The Perfect Foil is an American alternative rock supergroup formed in 2010 by singer Steve Taylor with drummer Peter Furler (Newsboys, Peter Furler Band), guitarist Jimmy Abegg (Vector, A Ragamuffin Band), and bassist John Mark Painter (Fleming and John).
Taylor formerly saw success as a new wave singer during the 1980s and early 1990s, and also fronted the short-lived band Chagall Guevara.
However, by the mid-1990s he abandoned performing music and transitioned into work as a record producer, songwriter, and film-maker.

In 2010 Taylor, who was frustrated at the slow pace of his fundraiser for his feature film "Blue Like Jazz", collaborated with Furler, Abegg, and Painter to record the song "A Life Preserved" for the film's soundtrack, and began recording other material.
Taylor, Abegg, and Painter then featured on the Peter Furler song "Closer", under the name "Steve Taylor & Some Other Band", on Furler's album "On Fire".
With those collaborations as a starting point, Taylor relaunched his career as a performing musician, and the lineup was branded as Steve Taylor & The Perfect Foil.
The group released a studio album entitled "Goliath" on November 18, 2014 to critical acclaim and modest commercial success.
In 2015, Daniel Smith of Danielson, with whom the band had been touring, temporarily joined on in a collaboration touted as Steve Taylor & The Danielson Foil.
Under this incarnation, the group released the EP "Wow to the Deadness" on February 5, 2016, and toured until February 12.

The Postal Service were an American indie rock band from Seattle, Washington, consisted of vocalist Ben Gibbard (of Death Cab for Cutie), producer Jimmy Tamborello (of Dntel and Headset), with Jenny Lewis (of Rilo Kiley) on background vocals.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- When the context contains a concise bridge claim about a named entity and at least one passage provides an explicit, identity-aligned statement linking that same named entity (or a clearly indicated alias) to the asserted relation, append exactly one brief corroborating sentence that paraphrases that passage's linking fact and references the corroborating passage. Perform an explicit identity-alignment check (alias or explicit linking); do not treat mere name co-occurrence or a passage that merely mentions the name as sufficient. Do not append this corroboration if the `context` already contains the identical corroborating fact verbatim. Keep this addition short and keep the overall summary compact.
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.
- When the summaries contain both a short single-token alias and, in the later integrated summary (summary_2), a contiguous multi-token full-name literal that explicitly identifies or is explicitly linked to the same person (for example via an identity link or biographical framing in summary_2), prefer and emit that multi-token full-name literal exactly as it appears in summary_2. Do not prefer the longer form unless summary_2 provides an explicit identity link tying the alias to the full-name literal; if no such explicit link is present, follow the base identity-link rules and do not force selection of the longer form.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: In between The Postal Service and Chagall Guevara which one was formed in 1989?
hop1_docs: Chagall Guevara | Chagall Guevara was an American rock band formed in 1989 by solo artist Steve Taylor, guitarists Dave Perkins and Lynn Nichols (from the 1970s Phil Keaggy band), bassist Wade Jaynes, and drummer Mike Mead.
Chagall Guevara (album) | Chagall Guevara is the title of the only full-length album by the band Chagall Guevara, released in 1991, on MCA Records.

List of postal codes in Brazil | This list of postal codes in Brazil covers Brazil's Código de Endereçamento Postal (CEP), which is the Brazilian postal code.  In 1993, the eight-digit form was introduced; prior to 1993, the Brazilian postal codes used the five-digit form.  Correios is the state-owned company which provides postal services in Brazil.
Liver (album) | Liver is a live album by Steve Taylor, released in 1995.  Its contents cover all of Taylor's career, including his time with Chagall Guevara.

Hrvatska pošta | Croatian Post Inc. (HP-Hrvatska pošta d.d.), founded in 1999, is a business corporation in ownership of the Republic of Croatia.  Croatian Post is a national postal operator of the Republic of Croatia.  Post offices of the Croatian Post (total number: 1016) form one of the widest services and retail networks in the country.  Apart from postal services Croatian Post offers also payment services, retail services and digital TV service in the whole territory of Croatia.  Croatian Post is also one of the founders and a full member of the Association of European Public Postal Operators (PostEurop), while Croatia is a member of the Universal Postal Union (UPU).  Croatian Post also issues postage stamps of Croatia.

Sectional center facility | A destination Sectional Center Facility (SCF) is a Processing and Distribution Center (P&DC) of the United States Postal Service (USPS) that serves a designated geographical area defined by one or more three-digit ZIP Code prefixes.  A Sectional Center Facility routes mail between local post offices and to and from Network Distribution Centers (NDCs), which form the backbone of the network.

Vectis postal service | The Vectis postal service was a postal service set up during the national postal strike of the United Kingdom in 1971.  This was amongst a number of local delivery services which flourished during the strike.
hop1_titles: List of postal codes in Brazil
response.summary_1: Passage [1] states: "Chagall Guevara was an American rock band formed in 1989" (explicitly gives Chagall Guevara's formation year). No passage provides any formation year for The Postal Service. Next hop: retrieve The Postal Service's formation/founding year (e.g., the "The Postal Service (band)" page) to compare which band was formed in 1989.
response.hop2_query: The Postal Service band formation year
hop2_docs: The Postal Service discography | The discography of North American indie band The Postal Service consists of one studio album, one live album, seven singles and five music videos.

Somali Postal Service | The Somali Postal Service (Somali Post) is the national postal service of Somalia.  It is part of the Federal Government of Somalia's Ministry of Information, Posts and Telecommunications.  The postal authority historically sent mail to and received parcels from various domestic and international destinations, but shut down operations in 1991.  Somali Post later officially relaunched on 1 November 2013, following a Memorandum of Understanding signed with Emirates Post in April of the year.  A new postal coding and number system for each of Somalia's 18 administrative regions was also developed in October 2014.

Postage stamps and postal history of Puerto Rico | The postal history of Puerto Rico began 161 years ago when Puerto Rico issued postage stamps jointly with Cuba in 1856.  This would be followed by postal cards and telegraph stamps, until the United States stamps used today on the island as it is a territory serviced by the United States Postal Service (USPS).

Postal services in Andorra | Postal services in Andorra are unique in that they are not operated by the country itself, but by its two larger neighbouring countries, Spain and France.  This is a legacy of the centuries of "de facto" control exercised by those two countries over Andorra. " Correos" of Spain and "La Poste" of France operate side by side.  However the postal code system, introduced in July 2004, has a different format from those of either Spain or France, consisting of the letters "AD", followed by three digits.

Postage stamps and postal history of Turkey | The postal history of Turkey and its predecessor state, the Ottoman Empire, dates to the 18th century when foreign countries maintained courier services through their consular offices in the Empire.  Although delayed in the development of its own postal service, in 1863 Turkey became the second independent country in Asia (after Russia) to issue adhesive postage stamps, and in 1875, it became a founding member of the General Postal Union, soon to become the Universal Postal Union.  Turkey became a republic in 1923, and in the following years, its postal service became more modernized and efficient and its postage stamps expertly designed and manufactured.

The Postal Service | The Postal Service were an American indie rock band from Seattle, Washington, consisted of vocalist Ben Gibbard (of Death Cab for Cutie), producer Jimmy Tamborello (of Dntel and Headset), with Jenny Lewis (of Rilo Kiley) on background vocals.

Postal codes in Norway | Norwegian Postal Codes are 4-digit codes, known in Norwegian as "postnummer" (literally "post number").  Posten, the Norwegian postal service, makes small modifications to the postal code system each year.  In 1999 Posten made considerable changes to the postal codes in Norway.
hop2_titles: Postage stamps and postal history of Puerto Rico
Postage stamps and postal history of Turkey
response.summary_2: Chagall Guevara was formed in 1989. The retrieved passages do not provide a formation year for The Postal Service.
